name: branch
context: Create, switch, rename, delete, and list git branches for parallel development workflows
info: Branches are git's mechanism for parallel development. A branch is a lightweight movable pointer to a commit — creating one is instant and costs almost nothing. This skill covers creating branches from any ref, switching between branches with checkout and switch, listing local and remote branches, renaming and deleting branches, and understanding tracking relationships with upstream branches. Agents use branches constantly to isolate feature work, bug fixes, and experiments. The default branch (usually main) is the integration target. Feature branches diverge from main and eventually merge back via [[git/merge]] or [[git/rebase]]. Remote-tracking branches (origin/main) mirror the state of branches on [[git/remote]] repositories.
body:
# Git Branch

Branches let you work on multiple things in parallel. Creating a branch is instant — it just writes a 40-byte commit hash to a file in `.git/refs/heads/`.

## Creating Branches

```bash
# Create a branch at current HEAD
git branch feature-auth

# Create and switch to it in one step
git checkout -b feature-auth

# Modern alternative (git 2.23+)
git switch -c feature-auth

# Create from a specific commit or branch
git branch hotfix-login origin/main
git checkout -b hotfix-login origin/main

# Create from a tag
git checkout -b release-prep v2.1.0
```

## Switching Branches

```bash
# Switch to existing branch
git checkout main
git switch main  # modern alternative

# Switch and discard local changes (dangerous)
git checkout -f main

# Switch back to previous branch
git checkout -
git switch -
```

**Warning**: Switching branches with uncommitted changes will fail if those changes conflict with the target branch. Either [[git/commit]] or [[git/stash]] your changes first.

## Listing Branches

```bash
# List local branches (* marks current)
git branch

# List remote-tracking branches
git branch -r

# List all branches (local + remote)
git branch -a

# List with last commit info
git branch -v

# List branches merged into current
git branch --merged

# List branches NOT merged into current
git branch --no-merged

# List branches containing a specific commit
git branch --contains abc1234
```

## Renaming Branches

```bash
# Rename current branch
git branch -m new-name

# Rename a specific branch
git branch -m old-name new-name

# Rename and update remote
git branch -m old-name new-name
git push origin :old-name new-name
git push origin -u new-name
```

## Deleting Branches

```bash
# Delete a merged branch
git branch -d feature-done

# Force delete an unmerged branch
git branch -D feature-abandoned

# Delete a remote branch
git push origin --delete feature-done

# Clean up stale remote-tracking branches
git remote prune origin
# or
git fetch --prune
```

## Tracking Branches

```bash
# Set upstream for current branch
git branch -u origin/main

# Create branch tracking a remote
git checkout --track origin/feature-x

# See tracking info
git branch -vv
```

Tracking branches enable `git pull` and `git push` without specifying the remote and branch name. When you clone, `main` automatically tracks `origin/main`.

## Branch Naming Conventions

| Pattern | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `main` | Primary integration branch |
| `develop` | Development integration (gitflow) |
| `feature/<name>` | Feature development |
| `fix/<name>` or `bugfix/<name>` | Bug fixes |
| `hotfix/<name>` | Urgent production fixes |
| `release/<version>` | Release preparation |
| `chore/<name>` | Maintenance tasks |

## Agent Best Practices

1. **Always branch from the latest main** — `git checkout -b feature main && git pull origin main`
2. **Use descriptive names** — `feature/add-oauth` not `my-branch`
3. **Delete after merge** — keep the branch list clean
4. **Check branch status** — `git branch -vv` shows ahead/behind counts
5. **Don't delete unmerged work** — use `-d` (safe) not `-D` (force) unless intentional

sections.title: Creating branches
sections.body: Create with `git branch <name>` (creates only) or `git switch -c <name>` / `git checkout -b <name>` (creates and switches). Create from any ref: `git switch -c hotfix origin/main`, `git switch -c release v2.0`. The new branch points to the same commit as the source ref. No files are copied — branches are just pointers.

sections.title: Switching between branches
sections.body: Use `git switch <branch>` (git 2.23+) or `git checkout <branch>`. Use `git switch -` to go back to the previous branch. Switching fails if uncommitted changes conflict with the target — resolve by committing ([[git/commit]]) or stashing ([[git/stash]]). Use `git switch --discard-changes` to force (destroys local changes).

sections.title: Deleting branches
sections.body: Safe delete with `git branch -d <name>` — refuses if branch has unmerged commits. Force delete with `git branch -D <name>`. Delete remote branch with `git push origin --delete <name>`. Clean stale remote refs with `git fetch --prune`. Always verify with `git branch --merged` before bulk cleanup.

sections.title: Upstream tracking
sections.body: Set upstream with `git branch -u origin/<branch>` or `git push -u origin <branch>`. View tracking info with `git branch -vv`. Tracking enables argument-free `git pull` and `git push`. When you `git checkout <remote-branch>` and a matching remote-tracking branch exists, git auto-creates a local tracking branch.

related.note: Branches exist to diverge and merge — merge brings them back together
Rebase is an alternative to merge for integrating branch changes
Stash is needed when switching branches with uncommitted work
Worktrees let you have two branches checked out simultaneously without switching

scripts.name: Branch Manager
scripts.description: Create, list, or delete branches with safety checks
scripts.inline:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ACTION="${1:?Usage: branch-manager.sh <create|list|delete|cleanup> [args...]}"
shift

case "$ACTION" in
  create)
    NAME="${1:?Branch name required}"
    BASE="${2:-HEAD}"
    git switch -c "$NAME" "$BASE"
    echo "Created and switched to branch: $NAME (from $BASE)"
    git log --oneline -1
    ;;
  list)
    echo "=== Local branches ==="
    git branch -vv
    echo
    echo "=== Remote branches ==="
    git branch -r
    ;;
  delete)
    NAME="${1:?Branch name required}"
    CURRENT=$(git branch --show-current)
    if [ "$NAME" = "$CURRENT" ]; then
      echo "Error: Cannot delete the current branch. Switch first."
      exit 1
    fi
    if [ "$NAME" = "main" ] || [ "$NAME" = "master" ]; then
      echo "Error: Refusing to delete $NAME"
      exit 1
    fi
    git branch -d "$NAME"
    echo "Deleted branch: $NAME"
    ;;
  cleanup)
    echo "Branches merged into $(git branch --show-current):"
    git branch --merged | grep -v '\*\|main\|master' || echo "(none)"
    echo
    echo "Run 'git branch -d <name>' to delete merged branches."
    ;;
  *)
    echo "Unknown action: $ACTION"
    echo "Usage: branch-manager.sh <create|list|delete|cleanup> [args...]"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

base.description: Base ref to create from (default: HEAD)

author: dojo-community
